The project is divided into five phases with man-hours following a geometric progression. Let the first phase require x man-hours. The sequence would then be x, 2x, 4x, 8x, 16x. The total man-hours is the sum of these values. The sum of a geometric series is given by the formula S = a(r^n - 1)/(r - 1), where a is the first term, r is the common ratio, and n is the number of terms. Substituting the known values, we get 310 = x(2^5 - 1)/(2 - 1). Simplifying, 310 = x(32 - 1)/1. Therefore, x = 310/31 = 10. The man-hours required for each phase are 10, 20, 40, 80, and 160.

The initial timeline for the project was 20 weeks. Each phase will take 1.5 times longer than planned. The total project duration will be increased by the same factor. Therefore, the new total project duration is 20 * 1.5 = 30 weeks. The increase in time does not affect the order of the phases. The project manager must adjust the schedule accordingly to accommodate the extended timeline. The project manager must ensure that all teams are aware of the new timeline and adjust their work accordingly. The project manager must also monitor the progress of the project to ensure that it stays on track. The project manager must ensure that all resources are allocated efficiently. The project manager must also communicate effectively with all stakeholders. The project manager must ensure that all deliverables are completed on time. The project manager must also ensure that all risks are managed effectively. The project manager must ensure that all quality standards are met. The project manager must also ensure that all team members are motivated and engaged. The project manager must ensure that all team members are working towards the common goal. The project manager must also ensure that all team members are receiving the necessary support. The project manager must ensure that all team members are working efficiently. The project manager must also ensure that all team members are working effectively. The project manager must ensure that all team members are working collaboratively. The project manager must also ensure that all team members are working in harmony. The project manager must ensure that all team members are working towards the success of the project.
